description: Apply regression extensions — influence analysis (leverage, studentised residuals, Cook's D), dummy variables (intercept and slope), and qualitative dependents (logit, probit)

influence_analysis.leverage: Hat matrix diagonal h_ii; high leverage if h_ii > 3*(k+1)/n
influence_analysis.studentised_residuals: Residual / its estimated SE; |t*| > 3 flags outlier
influence_analysis.cooks_distance: D_i = (r_i² / (k+1)) * (h_ii/(1-h_ii)²); D_i > 1 (or > 0.5 cautious) flags influential observation
influence_analysis.detection_rule: An observation is influential when removing it materially changes the fitted coefficients

dummy_variables.intercept_dummy: 0/1 indicator that shifts intercept for category
dummy_variables.slope_dummy: Interaction term X*D allowing slope to vary by category
dummy_variables.n_minus_1_rule: For n categories, use n-1 dummies to avoid the dummy variable trap

qualitative_dependents.logit: ln(p/(1-p)) = b0 + b1X1 + …; coefficients interpreted as log-odds change
qualitative_dependents.probit: Φ⁻¹(p) = b0 + b1X1 + …; uses standard normal CDF link

errors.message: extension_type must be influence, dummy, logit, or probit

anti_patterns.rule: Do not include n dummies for n categories
anti_patterns.why: Causes perfect multicollinearity with the intercept (dummy variable trap); always use n-1 dummies and interpret omitted as baseline

anti_patterns.rule: Do not delete influential observations without investigation
anti_patterns.why: Influential points may signal genuine regime change or data error; mechanical deletion biases the model and hides real-world signal

anti_patterns.rule: Do not fit OLS to a binary dependent variable
anti_patterns.why: Linear probability model produces fitted values outside [0,1] and heteroskedastic residuals; logit or probit are correct specifications
